name: Neo4j MCP Servers
what_it_does: A suite of four MCP servers that enable AI assistants to interact with Neo4j graph databases via natural language, covering Cypher query execution, persistent knowledge graph memory, Aura cloud instance management, and interactive data modeling.
use_cases: Query a Neo4j graph database using natural language instead of writing Cypher
Build and maintain a persistent knowledge graph that survives across AI conversations
Manage Neo4j Aura cloud instances (create, delete, scale) through an AI assistant
Design and validate graph data models with Arrows.app visualization integration
Generate analytics and visualizations from Neo4j data
not_for: Teams without an existing Neo4j instance or Aura account
Production workloads — this is a Neo4j Labs experimental project
best_when: You already use Neo4j and want an AI assistant to query, model, or manage it without writing Cypher manually.
avoid_when: You need a stable, production-grade integration; the Labs label means APIs and behavior may change.

auth.notes: Neo4j credentials (host, user, password) for Cypher/memory servers; Aura API credentials (client ID/secret) for the cloud management server. APOC plugin required for schema inspection.

pricing.notes: The MCP servers are MIT-licensed and free. Neo4j itself has free Community Edition and paid Enterprise; Aura has a free tier and paid plans.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error handling depends on underlying Neo4j driver responses; no custom agent-friendly error wrapping documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations are idempotent; write Cypher operations are not guaranteed idempotent depending on query.
known_agent_gotchas: APOC plugin must be installed separately on the Neo4j instance for schema introspection to work
Experimental Labs project — breaking changes possible without notice
Natural language to Cypher translation can produce incorrect queries on complex graph schemas
